prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arr matey, gather 'round an' let ol' Cap'n Redbeard tell ye the tale o' the blue heavens! Ye see, when the sun's rays besailin' our good Earth, they encounters the air, thick wit' little particles and gassy concoctions. Now, these rays be a mix o' all the colors o' the rainbow, but the blue light, bein' the craftiest, scatters in all directions by them air particles. It's like a treasure hunt, whar the blue plunder be spread all over the sky! So, when ye be gazin' up, it's the blue light what be catchin' yer eye from every corner o' the heavens. That's why, me hearty, the sky be as blue as the deepest sea!
